steps.title: E2E pre-deploy P1: last_event_ts populated within 11s
steps.classification: code-defect (pre-existing, cross-cutting, surfaced by this VP)
steps.output: New session sess-9187066b-37c8-4a5b-a849-f3a637cba167 created via UI at 13:53:55Z. Runner PID 3112926 spawned and dial-back WS connected. SessionDO local session_meta SQLite shows correct terminal state (sdk_session_id=82219318, status=idle, duration_ms=7713, num_turns=2, cost=0.2004775). BUT D1 agent_sessions row still shows status='running', num_turns=NULL, duration_ms=NULL, total_cost_usd=NULL, last_event_ts=NULL, updated_at == created_at 60+ seconds after completion. Root cause: session-do.ts:1662 uses this.ctx.id.toString() (64-hex idFromName hash) as the WHERE clause, but since commit 2af282c agent_sessions.id is 'sess-<uuid>' (client-supplied). UPDATE matches 0 rows. Pattern repeats at session-do.ts lines 1403, 1442, 1463, 1483, 1508, 1546, 1590, 1616, 1662, 1727, 1840, 3013.

steps.title: Post-deploy prod VP (SIGKILL runner + D1 query)
steps.output: Manual step per spec lines 235–246; executed only after PR#52 merges and infra pipeline deploys.

blocking_findings: DO → D1 id mismatch: flushLastEventTsToD1 (and ~12 other agent_sessions writers in session-do.ts) target this.ctx.id.toString() while D1 PKs are the client-supplied 'sess-<uuid>' strings since commit 2af282c. Flush is a silent no-op in every flow that uses client_session_id. Spec B2's primary success criterion (D1 last_event_ts populated) is unobservable.
non_blocking_findings: use-now.tsx deviates from spec Gotchas (L391) on SSR/hydration safety — uses Date.now() as both createContext default and useState initializer instead of 0.
Spec P1 task 5 naming rename not applied — session-do.ts:233 still reads LAST_EVENT_FLUSH_DEBOUNCE_MS; surrounding JSDoc still says 'debounce'.
recommendation: Do not merge PR#52 until the DO→D1 id source is reconciled (either fix session-do.ts writers to use this.state.session_id or equivalent, or confirm + document that prod uses a path where this.ctx.id.toString() === agent_sessions.id). The PR's code is internally correct (unit-tested predicate, migration, reader swap) but its end-to-end value (flipping stuck-running sessions to Idle via D1-driven TTL) cannot be observed for sessions created through the current UI.
